---
title: "Comparison_Vessel_Diameter_Pericyte_Length_Fluorescence_Change"
author: "Mirja grote Lambers"
date: "2026-05-03"
output: html_document
---

This script calculates descriptive statistics (mean ± SEM, median, SD, n) and assesses normality (Shapiro-Wilk, histogram, QQ-plot) of the percentage change in pericyte vessel diameter, pericyte length, and fluorescence intensity across five experimental conditions.
Whether values changed systematically from baseline is tested using a nonparametric studentized permutation test for paired data (npar.t.test.paired, nparcomp, Konietschke & Pauly, 2012).

H0: The before and after measurements do not differ systematically (relative effect p = 0.5).

H1: The before and after measurements differ systematically (relative effect p ≠ 0.5, two-sided).


# load data
df <- read.csv("Comparison_Vessel_Diameter_Pericyte_Length_Fluorescence_Change.csv")

# pre-compute background-corrected fluorescence
df$fluorescence_corrected_t1     <- df$fluorescence_intensity_t1_whole_cell - df$background_t1
df$fluorescence_corrected_t6_t12 <- df$fluorescence_intensity_t6_t12_whole_cell - df$background_t6_t12

# experimental groups
groups <- c("control",
            "current injection to 0 mV",
            "current injection to -20 mV",
            "Recurrent depolarization steps",
            "200 nM U46619")

# parameters: col for descriptives, before/after for test
params <- list(
  list(label  = "vessel diameter",
       col    = "vessel_diameter_change_.",
       before = "vessel_diameter_t0",
       after  = "vessel_diameter_t6_t12"),
  list(label  = "pericyte length",
       col    = "pericyte_length_change_.",
       before = "pericyte_length_t1",
       after  = "pericyte_length_t6_t12"),
  list(label  = "fluorescence",
       col    = "fluorescence_change_whole_cell_.",
       before = "fluorescence_corrected_t1",
       after  = "fluorescence_corrected_t6_t12")
)

# normality check
check_normality <- function(x, name) {
  sw <- shapiro.test(x)
  cat(sprintf("%s – Shapiro-Wilk: W = %.3f, p = %.3f\n", name, sw$statistic, sw$p.value))
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
  hist(x, main = paste("Histogram –", name), xlab = "% change",
       col = "steelblue", border = "white")
  qqnorm(x, main = paste("QQ-Plot –", name))
  qqline(x, col = "red")
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}

for (g in groups) {
  cat(sprintf("\n%s\n", toupper(g)))
  exp_data <- df[df$experiment == g, ]

  for (p in params) {
    x <- na.omit(exp_data[[p$col]])
    n <- length(x)

    sd_val <- sd(x)
    sem    <- sd_val / sqrt(n)
    ci     <- qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * sem

    cat(sprintf("\n  %s (n = %d)\n",                      p$label, n))
    cat(sprintf("    Mean ± SEM     = %.2f ± %.2f%%\n",   mean(x), sem))
    cat(sprintf("    95%% CI (mean)  : [%.2f, %.2f]%%\n", mean(x) - ci, mean(x) + ci))
    cat(sprintf("    Median         = %.2f%%\n",           median(x)))
    cat(sprintf("    SD             = %.2f%%\n",           sd_val))

    check_normality(x, paste(g, "-", p$label))

    df_long <- data.frame(
      value     = c(exp_data[[p$before]], exp_data[[p$after]]),
      timepoint = factor(rep(c("before", "after"), each = n),
                         levels = c("before", "after"))
    )

    set.seed(42)
    np <- npar.t.test.paired(value ~ timepoint, data = df_long,
                            alternative = "two.sided",
                            info        = FALSE,
                            plot.simci  = FALSE,
                            rounds      = 6)
    summary(np)
    plot(np)
  }
}
